indication: Confirmed histological diagnosis of inoperable locally advanced or metastatic renal cell carcinoma with a clear cell component where the patient has received only one VEGF targeted systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic renal cancer, has progressed on previous treatment or within six months of stopping previous treatment, has not received either everolimus or lenvatinib, and either has no brain metastases or brain metastases that are symptomatically stable. WHO Performance status 0, 1. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take at the same time each day consistently with or without food. Tablets swallowed whole. Available as 2.5mg, 5mg and 10mg tablets; dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg (up if halfway). Supply one original pack per 28-day cycle. Ensure correct brand for indication.

Take at the same time each day consistently with or without food. Capsules swallowed whole. If a dose is missed and cannot be taken within 12 hours it should be missed and the next dose taken at the appropriate time. Available as 10mg and 4mg hard capsules. Supply one original pack per 28-day cycle. Ensure correct brand for indication. Dose reduction levels: recommended 18mg, 1st reduction 14mg, 2nd reduction 10mg, 3rd reduction 8mg.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below then reduce everolimus to 2.5mg once a day or discontinue

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or recurrence of grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until symptoms resolved to grade 1 or below then restart at 2.5mg once a day

Interrupt therapy. Prescribe prednisolone 40mg as indicated. Restart at 2.5mg daily when resolved to grade 1 or below or discontinue
Interrupt everolimus until resolved. Restart at 5mg once a day

dose_modifications.condition: Fasting glucose greater than 27.8mmol/L or triglycerides greater than 11.4mmol/L

dose_modifications.condition: Systolic BP >=160mmHg or diastolic BP >=100mmHg despite optimal antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold lenvatinib until systolic BP <=150mmHg and diastolic <=95mmHg and stable antihypertensive for at least 48 hours, then resume at a reduced dose level

dose_modifications.condition: Life-threatening hypertension (malignant hypertension, neurological deficit, hypertensive crisis)
dose_modifications.notes: Urgent intervention indicated. Discontinue lenvatinib and institute appropriate medical management
Interrupt treatment until less than 2g/24 hours, then dose reduce down a level and resume. Stop lenvatinib if nephrotic syndrome occurs.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Platelets minimum 75x10^9/L for everolimus. Proteinuria must be less than 1g/24 hours prior to each cycle. Blood pressure should be well controlled prior to starting treatment.

Blood glucose levels at baseline and after 28 days of treatment, thereafter every 4-8 weeks
Triglycerides at baseline then every 8 weeks

Thyroid function every 28 days
Blood pressure at baseline, after one week of lenvatinib then every two weeks for the first two months then every 28 days thereafter
ECG as clinically indicated
Liver function tests before initiation then every 2 weeks for first 2 months and monthly thereafter
Monitor LVEF for signs and symptoms of cardiac dysfunction
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed
Everolimus and lenvatinib must only be prescribed under supervision of an oncologist
Always check for drug interactions with both agents
Several brands of everolimus and lenvatinib exist; ensure correct product for indication is dispensed
No treatment breaks of more than 6 weeks beyond expected cycle length allowed
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped
Dose reductions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval
QT prolongation risk with lenvatinib; not recommended in congenital long QT syndrome; stop if QT >500ms
Lenvatinib should be discontinued if arterial thromboembolic event occurs

DPD deficiency testing required before cycle 1.

indication: Metastatic Germ Cell Tumours. Salvage/potentially curative treatment for germ cell tumours.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (20mg/ml). Do NOT dose cap at 2.4m². Extravasation classification: irritant.

Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride over 90 minutes. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (1mg/ml). Do NOT dose cap at 2.4m². Pre-hydration with 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 8mmol magnesium sulphate over 30 minutes. Post-hydration incorporated into ifosfamide/mesna administration. Extravasation classification: exfoliant.

Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes (co-infused with mesna 600mg/m²). Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (80mg/ml). Do NOT dose cap at 2.4m². Urine dip test for blood every 4 hours the day of and day after ifosfamide administration. Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours. Urine output maintained above 100ml/hour. Patients encouraged to drink 1-2L additional fluids. Extravasation classification: neutral.

pre_medications.timing: prior to ifosfamide, days 1-5
co-infused with ifosfamide over 60 minutes, days 1-5
post-ifosfamide over 240 minutes (4 hours), days 1-5
once a day on day 1 prior to chemotherapy
once a day on days 2, 3 prior to chemotherapy
once a day on days 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
three times a day when required for nausea, oral
twice a day on days 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
cisplatin pre-hydration over 30 minutes each day
twice a day for 7 days starting on day 8 of cycle
30 million units once daily for 7 days from day 6 (if <80kg), 48 million units if >=80kg; or lenograstim 33.6 million units once daily for 7 days from day 6; or pegfilgrastim 6mg once on day 6
pre_medications.indication: Growth factor support according to local policy

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 1x10^9/L prior to cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Eligible level: neutrophils equal to or more than 1x10^9/L. Delay in 3-day increments. Dose delays rather than dose reductions recommended.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets less than 100x10^9/L prior to cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Eligible level: platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L. Delay in 3-day increments.

dose_modifications.condition: Ifosfamide: bilirubin greater than ULN or AST greater than 2.5xULN or ALP greater than 2.5xULN
Etoposide: bilirubin 26-51 micromol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Etoposide: bilirubin greater than 51 micromol/L or AST/ALT greater than 180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: May reduce ifosfamide dose for next cycle

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 neurological toxicity or worsening despite dose reduction
dose_modifications.notes: Stop ifosfamide. Methylene blue 50mg four times a day IV in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes can be used to prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Inpatient regimen. FBC, LFTs and U&Es (including magnesium, phosphate and calcium) prior to each cycle. Serum albumin prior to each cycle. EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance. AFP and HCG on day 1. Urine dip test for blood every 4 hours on day of and day after ifosfamide. Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours. Urine output above 100ml/hour required.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es (including magnesium, phosphate and calcium) prior to each cycle
Serum albumin prior to each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance
Urine dip test for blood every 4 hours the day of and day after ifosfamide administration
Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours the day of and day after ifosfamide administration
Urine output maintained above 100ml/hour
AFP and HCG on day 1 of each cycle
special_warnings: INPATIENT regimen - all supportive and take-home medication must be prescribed on inpatient chart (not on Aria)
VIP is a salvage/potentially curative regimen for metastatic germ cell tumours
Do NOT dose cap regimen at 2.4m² - override any caps in Aria (potentially curative treatment)
Mesna uroprotection mandatory with ifosfamide: pre-dose 120mg/m² bolus, concurrent 600mg/m² with ifosfamide, post-dose 600mg/m² over 4 hours
Haemorrhagic cystitis risk with ifosfamide - urine monitoring essential
Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: methylene blue 50mg QDS IV can prevent/treat; stop ifosfamide if grade 2 neurotoxicity
Risk factors for CNS toxicity: low albumin, renal impairment, prior cisplatin, poor PS, CNS tumour, bulky pelvic disease, concomitant psychotropic drugs, younger age
Growth factor support required according to local policy from day 6
Patients to drink 1-2L additional fluids; maintenance bag 100ml/hour if unable
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped
Dose delays rather than dose reductions recommended (potentially curative intent)

required_patient_fields.renal_function: EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required

drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (20mg/ml). Do NOT dose cap at 2.4m². Extravasation classification: irritant.
drugs.notes: days 1-5 per cycle | Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (20mg/ml). Do NOT dose cap at 2.4m². Extravasation classification: irritant.

drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride over 90 minutes. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (1mg/ml). Do NOT dose cap at 2.4m². Pre-hydration with 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 8mmol magnesium sulphate over 30 minutes. Post-hydration incorporated into ifosfamide/mesna administration. Extravasation classification: exfoliant.

Extravasation classification: exfoliant.

Patients encouraged to drink 1-2L additional fluids. Extravasation classification: neutral.
